evals.name: cli-new-readme
evals.prompt: I just finished building a CLI tool that converts markdown files to PDF. Can you write a README for it?
evals.expected_output: A well-structured README for the md2pdf CLI tool with project description, install instructions, usage examples with common flags, and configuration options.
assertions: Has a clear project description in the first paragraph explaining what md2pdf does
Has a Quick Start or Getting Started section
Shows an install command (pip install or similar)
Includes usage examples with actual CLI flags (--theme, --toc, --watch, -o)
Mentions the available themes (default, dark, academic, minimal)
Has code blocks with realistic command-line examples

evals.name: library-improve-readme
evals.prompt: Our README is pretty outdated and thin — can you improve it?
evals.expected_output: An improved README for the datakit library that adds proper description, real usage examples showing schemas/validation/transforms, API overview, and development setup.
assertions: Substantially longer and richer than the original 12-line README
Has real code examples showing Schema usage (parse, safeParse, or refine)
Shows validation or transform usage examples
Documents the public API surface or main exports (Schema, validate, transform, pipe, types)
Has development setup section (how to run tests, build, or contribute)

evals.name: internal-app-readme
evals.prompt: We need a README for this internal dashboard app. It's a React + FastAPI project our team uses to monitor deployments.
evals.expected_output: A README for the deploy-dashboard internal tool covering what it does, how to run it locally (docker-compose), environment variables, development setup for both frontend and backend, and architecture overview.
assertions: Explains what the dashboard monitors (deployments across environments)
Documents how to run locally, mentioning docker-compose
Has development setup covering both frontend and backend
Mentions the tech stack (React, FastAPI, PostgreSQL)
